# Agents

Three specialist personas for GitHub Copilot Chat. Each is an expert in one domain. Use them directly or compose them via slash commands.

## When to use which agent

| Situation | Agent |
|-----------|-------|
| About to open a PR | `@code-reviewer` |
| Got a bug report | `@test-engineer` — reproduce it first |
| Writing a new feature | `@test-engineer` — write tests first |
| Touching auth, input handling, file uploads | `@security-auditor` |
| Code generated by another AI | `@code-reviewer` then `@security-auditor` |
| Unsure if test suite is good enough | `@test-engineer` — coverage analysis |

## Invoking agents

```
@code-reviewer Review the changes in src/api/users.ts
@test-engineer Write tests for the validateEmail function — use the Prove-It pattern
@security-auditor Audit the file upload handler in src/routes/upload.ts for OWASP vulnerabilities
```

## Composition rules

Agents are **independent specialists**. They do not call each other.

- If `@code-reviewer` flags something that needs deeper security investigation → you invoke `@security-auditor` separately
- If `@security-auditor` recommends adding tests → you invoke `@test-engineer` separately
- Orchestration is your job (or a slash command's job) — not the agent's

This is intentional. An agent that delegates to another agent is unpredictable and hard to debug.

## Adding a new agent

Create a `.md` file in this directory with valid frontmatter:

```markdown
---
name: your-agent-name
description: One sentence describing what it does and when to invoke it
---

# Agent Title

[System prompt for the agent. Be specific about its role, constraints, and output format.]
```

Copilot discovers it automatically via the `.github/agents/` convention. No registration or config file needed.

## Agent files in this repo

| File | Persona | Specialty |
|------|---------|-----------|
| `code-reviewer.md` | Staff Engineer | 5-axis review: correctness, readability, arch, security, perf |
| `test-engineer.md` | QA Engineer | TDD, Prove-It pattern, coverage analysis |
| `security-auditor.md` | Security Engineer | OWASP Top 10, STRIDE, threat modeling |
